Football.title: World Cup’s sexiest fan Ivana Knoll makes surprise announcement as she reveals half-time DJ set for major upcoming match
Football.content:
IVANA KNOLL has made a stunning announcement ahead of the World Cup this summer.

The football fan shot to fame with her appearances at the tournament in Qatar four years ago.





Ivana, 33, caught the attention of millions for her love of Croatia which she showed with her stunning outfits.

The brunette became a social media star and now boasts almost 3million followers on Instagram.

She has recently taken up a new career as a DJ and has enjoyed immediate success.

She has performed across Europe, the Middle East and in North America under her stage name KnollDoll.



But now she is set for her biggest show of to date after being announced as a performer at this ahead of this summer’s World Cup.

Ivana revealed that she will be playing a DJ set during the half-time of the match between he beloved Croatia and Colombia.

The friendly match is scheduled for March 26 at the Camping World Stadium in Orlando, Florida.

Ivana shared the news of her booking on her Instagram account for her horde of followers.



She wrote: “Dream come true.

“Playing the Half-time Show for Croatia vs Colombia at Camping World Stadium in Orlando.

“Performing for my country has always been my biggest dream.”

Fans have filled the comment section as they congratulated her on the achievement.

One posted: “This is incredible. So happy for you.”



A second wrote: “Wow, very exciting.”



A third commented: “This is massive, so happy and proud of you.”

Both sides have qualified for the World Cup with Croatia facing England in their opening game on June 17.

They will then take on Panama and Ghana as they aim for a spot in the knockout rounds.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Colombia face Uzbekistan and Portugal with the final team yet to be decided.

It will be between the DR Congo, Jamaica, or New Caledonia coming through the play-offs.

Football.title: Newcastle ace who has played more than anyone else in Premier League this term targets World Cup after snubbing Scotland
Football.content:
NO PREMIER LEAGUE star has played more games than Harvey Barnes this season.

And Newcastle’s winger would love a few more with England this summer after snubbing a World Cup adventure with Scotland.



The 28-year-old, capped once by the Three Lions in 2020 in a friendly against Wales, qualifies for the Tartan Army through his grandparents.



Barnes was approached by Steve Clarke last month regarding an international switch, but he revealed the conversation was short.

He said: “We had a chat in private. It wasn’t a big sort of thing, it was a quick chat and this was the decision I came out with.”

Barnes instead is trying to force his way into Thomas Tuchel’s plans – and scoring against the likes of Barcelona in the Champions League will do his cause no harm.

His strike, his 14th of the season, in Newcastle’s last-16 first leg 1-1 draw against the Catalans was his sixth goal in the Champions League this term.



Only Kylian Mbappe, Mags team-mate Anthony Gordon, Victor Osimhen, Julian Alvarez and Michael Olise’s have more goal contributions than Barnes (9) in this term.

The game against Barca was Barnes’ 47th appearance of the season in all competitions, the most of any top-flight player.

But the ace, signed for £38m from Leicester in 2023, is showing no signs of fatigue. Instead, he would snatch your hand off for at least another one for the Three Lions at this summer’s showpiece.

Cheltenham Festival: Get over £500 in free bets



He added: “Of course. Every player wants to be at the World Cup, I’m no different to any of them.

“Moments like Tuesday and with the games coming up, it’s about doing as well as I can, making sure my form is good at club level and you never know.

“All I can do is keep chipping in with goals and assists, help the team where I can and see where it takes me.”

Barnes hopes to sprinkle more magic in the Champions League by firing Newcastle to a famous victory at the Nou Camp on Wednesday and seal a place in the last-eight.

First he and the Mags head to West London to face Chelsea – a must-win game if they are to mount a late charge for Europe and climb up the table from 12th.



Though boss Eddie Howe admits he faces a tough task to ensure his players are mentally focused on the Blues ahead of their date with destiny at the Nou Camp.

He said: “Well, that’s the challenge for us. Is it going to be easy? Probably not for the players because naturally they’ll have half an eye on next week.

“They’re human beings, that will be the case. But we’ve got to try to make sure that the focus is firmly on Chelsea first. It’s a hard enough game as it is without not being 100 per cent committed and focused on the details of that game.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“That’s my job to try to make that clear to the players, and we need to back up the performance on Tuesday. We can’t let it slide.

“There was so much good in our performance, so much of us at our best. We need to have more of that on a consistent basis.”

Football.title: ‘Glitch in the Matrix’ – Fans left stunned by eerie optical illusion after Doku ‘DISAPPEARS’ during live TV broadcast
Football.content:
SOME would argue the Man City players went missing in their heavy defeat to Real Madrid – but one player literally did.

Jeremy Doku went viral for the bizarre moment in which he disappeared from TV screens.





The weird moment, quickly compared to sci-fi movie The Matrix, happened at the Bernabeu where City were thrashed 3-0 following a first-half hat-trick by Federico Valverde.

Trying to get City back into it, Belgium international Doku drove the ball down the left wing where he was marked by Real stars Valverde and Trent Alexander-Arnold.

Then suddenly the 23-year-old Man City star DISAPPEARED.



Football fans were left stunned as they took to social media.

One fan tweeted: “Glitch in the Matrix!”

Another commented: “Where did Jeremy Doku go here?!?”

A third joked: “He was doing an impression of a Tottenham defender.”



This fan said: “I thought I was the only one that saw that.”

BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

And that one added: “Twilight Zone!!!”

The bizarre moment could be down to the augmented reality advertising boards placed around the pitch.

The augmented reality system changes the pitch side adverts depending on which country it is being viewed, rather than the advert being completely generic.

It’s possible that Man City’s green kit and the technology caused a glitch that led to Doku’s moment of magic.

Football.title: Aston Villa star Douglas Luiz moves on from Alisha Lehmann as he goes public with Arsenal legend’s 19-year-old daughter
Football.content:
ASTON VILLA midfielder Douglas Luiz has swapped the “world’s sexiest footballer” Alisha Lehmann for a stunning teenage Brazilian model and influencer.

Leicester ace Lehmann, 27, has been treating her army of social media fans to glimpses of her new romance with semi-professional footballer and Love Island star Montel McKenzie after they went Instagram official in January.





And overnight Douglas, whose four-year relationship with his Swiss beauty ex ended last year, hit back by going public with his latest girlfriend – a 19-year-old London-based blonde called Duda Gaspar.

Duda’s dad is ex-Arsenal star Edu Gaspar, who played for the Brazil national team as well as Corinthians and Valencia and was appointed Global Head of Football at Nottingham Forest in July last year.

Luiz’s new squeeze posted a selfie photo on her Instagram overnight with her arms around the footballer, 27, who left Villa in the summer of 2024 for Juventus but returned 18 months later off the back of a Forest loan spell.

She told her 57,000 followers in a telltale message alongside the cute snap: “You are my greatest certainty” followed by a heart emoticon.

Douglas, in a separate message alongside the same picture taken in a bar, wrote: “Making you happy will be my priority.”

It was not immediately clear today how long the pair have been dating, and whether the Premier League player already knew Duda before his loan period with Nottingham Forest.

The model is expected to stay in the UK if her dad, facing the axe at Forest after he was reportedly told to stay away from the club’s training ground, ends up getting the sack and returning to Brazil.

Top-flight Brazilian side Flamengo have reportedly made contact as they consider potential replacements for sporting director Jose Boto.



Alisha Lehmann, known as the ‘world’s sexiest footballer’, began dating Douglas Luiz in 2021 after they met at Aston Villa where the stunning female footballer used to play.

They both completed transfers to Juventus in the summer of 2024, with Lehmann helping the women’s side win Serie A last season.



Reports in Italy at the time of her wild pitch celebrations suggested the headline-making couple, the first ever boyfriend and girlfriend transfer when they moved to Juve, had split up.

The forward took social media by storm last month with a loved-up Valentine’s Day post where she wore a red dress and held a number of red roses.

The previous month her new love Montel, who starred in both Love Island’s original series as well as the All Stars spin-off, uploaded a selection of photos showing him cosying up to the striker.

She left Juve after just one campaign but stayed in Italy as she swapped for Como.



She joined Leicester City on a two and a half year contract on January 22 this year.

Football.title: Bernardo Silva offered shock summer transfer exit as two former team-mates try to lure £300k-a-week Man City star away
Football.content:
TURKISH giants Galatasaray are leaning on former Manchester City duo Ilkay Gundogan and Leroy Sane to lure Bernardo Silva to the club this summer.

Silva has reportedly already made up his mind that this will be his last season at Manchester City, despite signs that the club would be willing to extend his stay.





The 31-year-old is the current club captain and earns a staggering £300,000 a week, SunSport understands.

City are open to talks that could lead to a deal, but the Portuguese star would likely have to lower his wages and possibly accept a reduced role in the team.

However, with Galatasaray willing to offer a longer contract on similar wages, there is real temptation for him to move to the Turkish league at this stage of his career.

His agent Jorge Mendes has reportedly already held preliminary talks, although those discussions have mainly been to test the waters and assess the financial figures involved.

Galatasaray are in the middle of an interesting project and have recently shown they can afford stars such as Victor Osimhen. They are keen on a player with the profile of Bernardo Silva, who has won multiple trophies and is still capable of dominating a league like the Turkish Super Lig.

The fact that he could arrive for free makes the situation even more intriguing, with Juventus also interested. However, according to reports in Italy, their financial challenges mean Galatasaray currently have the advantage.

Bernardo is also close friends with both Gundogan and Sane, having played with them at Manchester City, and he remains in close contact with the pair.

The Portuguese midfielder is still clearly one of Pep Guardiola’s most trusted players, as he started the Champions League Round of 16 match against Real Madrid at the Bernabeu on Wednesday night.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

He lasted 70 minutes as a brilliant Federico Valverde hat-trick put Madrid in a commanding position in the first leg.

City are also still in with a shot at the Premier League title, but that will largely depend on how Arsenal cope with the pressure. The Gunners currently sit comfortably in first place with 67 points, seven more than City, although Guardiola’s side still have a game in hand.

If this does turn out to be Bernardo’s final season, as expected, he would love to crown it with another trophy to round off what would be almost a decade of excellence at Manchester City.

Football.title: Alan Brazil misses talkSPORT Breakfast Show at Cheltenham again as health update issued
Football.content:
ALAN BRAZIL has missed the talkSPORT Breakfast Show at Cheltenham for the second day running.

The legendary broadcaster felt unwell so was unable to perform his duties.



The reason for his absence was confirmed by talkSPORT, who expect Brazil to return for Gold Cup day tomorrow.



Brazil, 66, hosted the breakfast show on Tuesday from Cheltenham alongside Ally McCoist.

Wednesday was his designated rest day.

But he is not yet back to full fitness and “needs a little more time to recuperate” before he returns to the airwaves with Ray Parlour.

Shebahn Aherne filled in for the Scot, as she did on Tuesday.

Opening the show at 6am, she said: “Surprise! You’re listening to talkSPORT Breakfast, it’s Shebahn Aherne in for Alan Brazil. He needs a little bit more rest today, don’t we all?

“But Alan will be back with you tomorrow morning, fit and firing for Gold Cup Day.”

Co-host Gabby Agbonlahor added: “He’s a legend of Cheltenham.

Cheltenham Festival: Get over £500 in free bets

“And the amount of people that will be excited to see him tomorrow, to hear him, all across the world.



“Hopefully he’s fine for the morning.”

Brazil has become synonymous with the Cheltenham Festival but this is not the first time he has not fulfilled his duties.

He was famously axed by the station in 2004 after going AWOL at Cheltenham.

Two years ago, he missed the final day of coverage with Virgin Radio host Chris Evans forced to step in last minute.

Brazil later clarified that absence was not down to alcohol but due to being tired after a busy week and an issue with his knee.

Football.title: England’s World Cup clash SAVED as billionaire steps in to resolve bitter £6million stadium row
Football.content:
ENGLAND’S World Cup clash with Ghana has been SAVED after a billionaire stepped in to resolve a bitter £6million stadium row.

SunSport reported how a major spat had broken out over funding for the staging of matches at the Gillette Stadium near Boston.





Local leaders and the stadium owners, Kraft Sports & Entertainment (KSE), were at loggerheads to stump up the £6m needed to cover security, safety, manpower and infrastructure costs for the tournament.

KSE – who own the NFL’s New England Patriots and MLS’ New England Revolutions teams who play at the Gillette – were initially unwilling to foot the bill, given the stadium was being handed over to Fifa for the 39 days of matches there during the tournament.

But the town of Foxborough and the non-profit Boston Soccer 2026 also did not want to cover the costs and were determined not to bring the town’s taxpayers into the dispute.

They even threatened to deny the stadium a licence to stage matches if a resolution was not found.



That would have meant Fifa would need to move the seven World Cup matches – including England vs Ghana and two Scotland games – away from the 63,815-capacity Gillette to a new location.

A board member described the whole saga as “bulls***” and “sad”.

Time was running out, too, with a Foxborough Select Board meeting on March 17 and the local officials giving that as a deadline to come up with the cash or see the licence application rejected.

But now a resolution looks to have been found as the Kraft Group, headed by multi-billionaire Robert Kraft, agreed to pay.



Cheltenham Festival: Get over £500 in free bets

A joint statement between the KSE, Boston Soccer 2026 and the Town of Foxborough confirmed an “understanding” had been reached.

Although the licence has not been issued, it appears the feud is over.

The statement, released on Wednesday night, said the town, its council and residents will not incur any costs.

It said: “[We] have reached an understanding collectively that will allow Boston Soccer 2026 and the Town of Foxborough to finalise the details needed to approve an event license at the March 17 public hearing and ensure a safe and successful Fifa World Cup 2026 tournament at Gillette Stadium this summer.

“As part of this arrangement, the Town of Foxborough will not incur any cost or financial burden related to the Fifa World Cup, with Boston Soccer 2026 providing advance funding for security-related capital expenditures and the full extent of deployment that public safety officials have determined is needed to execute the event with Kraft Sports + Entertainment’s backing.



“We look forward to moving forward together positively in our shared goals of providing the highest level of public safety for this historic event and delivering a global experience for our region, which will infuse the Commonwealth and Foxborough with an influx of new visitors and associated economic impact.”

Foxborough town chair Bill Yukna thanked Kraft personally for his intervention.

Yukna added: “On behalf of the Town of Foxborough, I’m very pleased to say that all of our funding concerns have been addressed.

“I want to personally thank KSE and Robert Kraft for his involvement in bringing the funding concerns to a resolution.

“We expect that any open issues in the license will be resolved before the March 17 public hearing and we look forward to a very successful and safe World Cup event.



“I also want to thank our Police Chief Michael Grace, our Fire Chief Michael Kelleher and Town Manager Paige Duncan for their many hours and dedication to getting us to the finish line in this issue.”

The opening game at the Gillette sees Scotland take on Haiti overnight on June 13 in their first World Cup game for 28 years.

Football.title: Jesse Lingard casually cycles through Brazil’s favelas after ex-Man Utd star’s Corinthians transfer
Football.content:
JESSE LINGARD has been spotted casually cycling through Brazil’s favelas as he settles into life at Corinthians.

The former Manchester United star looked relaxed and at ease as he strolled around as part of an official club video.





The 33-year-old, who has 32 England caps, has played his football outside of Europe of late with FC Seoul and is now in Brazil.

In the video the attacker can be seen interacting with children and locals.

At one point he even eats what appears to be a cracker before joining what looks to be a fan in his house.

The video then turns to the famous streets as Lingard gets involved with the ball at his feet.

The final scene cuts to the ex-Nottingham Forest and West Ham man reading a newspaper article about his arrival.

One fan is especially excited to see him play, writing: “Let’s speed up the paperwork to play soon!”



Another said: “JLingzz ma man all the best.”

A host of others posted GIFs of the new signing from during his days in the Premier League.

Cheltenham Festival: Get over £500 in free bets

Speaking about his decision to join Corinthains, he said: “First, I saw the fans at the airport chanting ‘Go Corinthians! Go Corinthians!’, you know?

“There’s a lot of passion from the fans, which is wonderful to see. The training centre here is incredible.

“And the players welcomed me with open arms, the coach, the director, it was great to meet them.



“My first impressions – very happy, I can’t wait to play.”

Lingard also shared a clip from the stands of his new fanbase and was clearly impressed with the atmosphere they created.

In the clip on his Instagram stories, they could be seen bouncing around all four sides of the stadium with the English man sharing the video alongside two fire emojis.

Football.title: Erling Haaland to Barcelona transfer talks opened as Man City hold Spain meeting
Football.content:
Erling Haaland to Barcelona transfer talks opened as Man City hold Spain meeting

Erling Haaland is under contract with Manchester City until 2034, but that hasn't stopped Barcelona from registering their interest ahead of the summer transfer window

View 2 Images Erling Haaland is a target for Barcelona (Image: Maria Gracia Jimenez/Soccrates/Getty Images)

Erling Haaland was the subject of talks between Manchester City and would-be Barcelona chiefs on Wednesday. Victor Font, who's aiming to become Barca's next president, intends to make City striker Haaland his top target if he beats the incumbent Joan Laporta in Sunday's election.

‌



Haaland, 25, signed a new contract with City last year which keeps him at the Etihad until 2034. But the Norwegian's future remains the subject of speculation, particularly as it's unknown whether Pep Guardiola will be in charge of City next season.

‌



Barcelona are looking to sign a striker this summer, with Robert Lewandowski on course to leave the La Liga champions when his contract expires in June. Haaland has long been touted as Laporta's dream signing, but potential successor Font is looking to get ahead of his rival.

‌



Catalan outlet Sport reported that two members of Font's team, Carles Planchart and Xavier Aguilar, held talks with City's Ferran Soriano and Hugo Viana in Madrid, where City later lost their Champions League round-of-16 first leg 3-0 against Real. The two parties discussed a clause which would give Barcelona a preferential buy option on Haaland.

FOLLOW OUR MAN CITY FB PAGE! Follow our Manchester City Mirror Football Facebook page

Real Madrid destroy Man City as Federico Valverde nets stunning Champions League hat-trick READ MORE :

Barcelona draw up Marcus Rashford back-up plan as two transfers eyed in case deal collapses READ MORE :

Any deal this summer would be difficult, as Barca continue to be financially restrained and still haven't completed their £1.25billion renovation of the Nou Camp. However, a member of Laporta's board recently insisted that Barcelona are 'ready' to sign the likes of Haaland and Atletico Madrid forward Julian Alvarez.

‌



Speaking to radio station Cadena SER, Joan Soler declared: "Yes, we could sign players like Julian Alvarez or Erling Haaland. These transfers will pay for themselves within five years, and Barcelona's finances are ready for it."

Content cannot be displayed without consent

Soler added: "At the end of the day, Barca must be very careful about the commitments it makes regarding its wage structure. We have set ourselves a limit, and we will not exceed it. We must sign players at market prices while keeping an eye on our wage structure."

‌



A new striker doesn't necessarily mean the end for Lewandowski, though. Laporta, who beat Font in Barca's last presidential election in 2021, made it clear earlier this week that he wants to keep the 37-year-old for one more season.

How much should Manchester City demand for Erling Haaland? Have your say in the comments section.

View 2 Images Erling Haaland could be Robert Lewandowski's successor at Barcelona (Image: Adria Puig/Anadolu Agency via Getty Images)

‌



"He's a very talented player and a prolific goalscorer," Laporta explained. "As a token of my appreciation, I'd offer him another year on his contract.

"He came at a very difficult time, and despite his track record at Bayern [Munich], he pushed hard to come to Barca. He gave up a lot. We'll see what he wants to do, but I'd like him to stay another year if he wants to."

Football.title: World Cup cancellations: Why tournament has been called off in the past as Iran withdraws
Football.content:
World Cup cancellations: Why tournament has been called off in the past as Iran withdraws

Following Iran's withdrawal from the 2026 World Cup, we're looking at other instances where the tournament was threatened by boycotts and cancellations

View 5 Images Iran's withdrawal from the 2026 World Cup has threatened to scupper the tournament (Image: AP)

Iran has shocked the footballing world by pulling out of the 2026 World Cup. The decision follows the killing of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and the start of a military conflict with the United States.

‌



Because the US are joint-hosts of the tournament, Iranian officials say it is "impossible" for their team to compete. Despite this crisis, however, FIFA has been quick to calm fears. Chief operating officer Heimo Schirgi insisted on Monday that the World Cup would go ahead as planned, in spite of Iran's withdrawal.

‌



He also insisted that the World Cup must remain a way to bring the world together, even during wartime. Meanwhile, FIFA President Gianni Infantino has assured the Iranians that they are still "welcome" to compete in the 48-nation tournament they qualified for.

‌



Nevertheless, Iran's sudden exit has many fans wondering about the past. While it is rare for a team to quit so close to the opening match, the World Cup has been hit by global politics before. Here, we look back at the history of the tournament - from the years it was cancelled during World War II to the famous boycotts of the 1960s and 70s - and explore how the world's biggest sports competition has survived even the most difficult moments in history.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

World Cup crisis deepens as row over England venue continues READ MORE :

Iran punishments for World Cup withdrawal and can FIFA replace them with another nation? READ MORE :

Cancellations

While the history of the World Cup is littered with instances of geo-political tension and global conflict, the tournament itself has only been cancelled twice. On both occasions, it was due to World War II, with both the 1942 and 1946 editions being abandoned.

‌



The 1942 tournament had already been thrown into uncertainty even before the war fully escalated. Nazi Germany had submitted a bid to host and were widely considered favourites, but their invasion of Poland in 1939 prompted FIFA to cancel the competition altogether.

Despite the war ending in 1945, the decision was taken not to stage the 1946 World Cup. Six years of global conflict had left much of the world - particularly Europe - in turmoil. Football infrastructure had been severely disrupted, with many clubs and national teams disbanded or suspended as players and resources were redirected toward the war effort.

As a result, international football’s showpiece event did not return until 1950, marking the end of a 12-year hiatus for the World Cup.

‌



View 5 Images Iran have announced their intention to withdraw from the 2026 World Cup (Image: Jia Haocheng - Pool/Getty Images)

Boycotts and withdrawals

While Iran have become the first nation to boycott the World Cup since the 1970s, tournament withdrawals are far from unprecedented. Here, we'll run through the list of boycotts:

1930 - Most European nations boycotted the inaugural World Cup in 1930, which was held in Uruguay, due to the three-week transatlantic journey required to get reach South America. Only four Europeans teams made the trip: France, Belgium, Romania and Yugoslavia.

‌



1934 - Uruguay boycotted the tournament in Italy in retaliation for the European teams that had skipped the 1930 event. As the first World Cup winners, Uruguay became the first - and still the only - champions not to defend their title.

1938 - Uruguay and Argentina withdrew after France was selected to host. Both countries had expected the tournament to alternate between Europe and South America every four years, and viewed the decision as a break from that informal understanding.

View 5 Images Uruguay boycotted the 1934 World Cup despite being defending champions (Image: Getty Images)

‌



1950 - India opted out of competing due to the costs of travel to the tournament, which was held in Brazil. Scotland also boycotted after refusing to participate as the 'second best' UK team, having finished runners-up to England in the 1949/50 British Home Championship (a now defunct tournament between the Home Nations).

1958 - Turkey, Indonesia, Egypt and Sudan all refused to take part in World Cup qualifying since they had to compete against Israel. Due to the tense political climate in the Middle East at the time, several teams refused to set foot on the pitch with the Israelis.

1966 - All African nations refused to enter the qualifiers after FIFA announced there would be no guaranteed spot in the tournament for nations from the continent. The protest proved effective: Africa was awarded its first automatic qualifying place for the 1970 tournament.

‌



1974 - The Soviet Union refused to play a World Cup play-off against Chile because the match was scheduled at Santiago's National Stadium, which had been used by the regime of anti-Communist General Augusto Pinochet as a detention centre following his military coup. Reports that political prisoners had been held - and in some cases executed - at the stadium led the USSR to refuse to play there.

View 5 Images No African teams took part in the 1966 World Cup (Image: Hulton Archive/Getty Images)

Bans

While some nations have chosen to withdraw from the World Cup, others have been prevented from taking part altogether. Over the years, FIFA and international authorities have issued bans for political reasons, rule violations and wider global conflicts. Here are some of the most notable cases:

‌



1950 - Germany and Japan were barred from the 1950 World Cup in the aftermath of World War II as part of the broader international effort to isolate the two defeated powers. German football later split into separate East and West German teams, and the country would not compete again as one until reunification in 1990.

1966-1990 - South Africa were banned from the World Cup for nearly three decades due to the country's Apartheid policies. It remains the longest ban in FIFA history.

View 5 Images Russia have been banned from the World Cup since 2022 (Image: Getty Images)

‌



1990 - Mexico were excluded from the 1990 tournament after officials were found to have fielded four overage players during a youth qualifying competition. FIFA responded with a sweeping sanction, banning all Mexican national teams from international competition for two years.

1994 - Chile were expelled from the qualification process after goalkeeper Roberto Rojas staged an incident during a match against Brazil, pretending to be struck by a firework. He had secretly used a razor blade concealed in his glove to cut his own forehead. Once the deception was uncovered, Rojas received a lifetime ban and Chile were barred from the 1994 World Cup.

1994 - Yugoslavia were banned from all sporting competition by the UN following the outbreak of the Yugoslav Wars in 1994. FIFA booted them out just weeks before the opening game of the 1994 World Cup.

Article continues below

2022 - 2026 - Russia have banned from World Cup competition ever since the 2022 invasion of Ukraine, preventing them from participating in qualifying for the 2022 and 2026 tournaments.

Football.title: Man Utd new stadium hit by further delays sending cost of project beyond £3BILLION
Football.content:
Man Utd new stadium hit by further delays sending cost of project beyond £3BILLION

Manchester United have plans to build a new stadium to replace Old Trafford but they are facing issues in buying the land whilst their original costings could be way off the mark

View 2 Images Man Utd have plans to build a new stadium - but it is set to cost them several billion (Image: PA)

Manchester United's new stadium is set to see its costs soar beyond the £3billion mark amid ongoing delays and disputes over the cost of the proposed land.

‌



The club's hierarchy, led by Sir Jim Ratcliffe, want to build a new stadium after years of criticism. Old Trafford, once the country's premier footballing venue, has been overtaken by numerous state-of-the-art grounds. As a result, it has been used as a stick to beat United with.

‌



A new stadium was original set to cost the club a mammoth £2bn, a figure that emerged when the plans were announced a year ago. But buying the land to build the venue has proved an issue with owners Freightliner wanting £400m - eight times the £50m offered by the Manchester outfit.

‌



A compulsory purchase order could set a price and progress things, but work on a new stadium could take a decade before it is finally finished.

Ratcliffe has ploughed money into the club's training base and wants to continue bolstering their infrastructure, but the Ineos chief has been warned that the cost of such projects is only increasing - and his original figure may be well off the mark.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



A stadium expert told The Sun: “The current cost of the stadium is already north of £3billion – and the price will continue to rise. These kinds of massive projects won’t get cheaper, they are only going to increase. There are a limited number of companies out there who can pull off these kind of huge stadium projects, for one thing. This process to complete the stadium will take ten years from now – at best.”

United have seen Tottenham build a new stadium, regarded as one of the best in Europe. Manchester City and Liverpool have both redeveloped their venues whilst Arsenal's is only 20 years old this year. A damning reflection on Old Trafford came when it was overlooked for Euro 2028 - with the Etihad chosen as the Manchester-based venue.

View 2 Images Sir Jim Ratcliffe has been warned that his original costings could be well off the mark (Image: PA)

‌



Former United defender Gary Neville, who previously claimed that Old Trafford had been "neglected for far too long", had formed part of a task force that was responsible for ensuring the stadium's regeneration. He was joined by Sebastian Coe, the Mayor of Greater Manchester, Andy Burnham, and the chief executive of Trafford Council, Sara Todd.

Ratcliffe had looked to lobby the government to help pay for a new stadium, insisting it would serve as a huge asset to the north west. But his criticism of the country's leaders will have done little to aid his case with the billionaire claiming recently the country had been "colonised by immigrants".

Football.title: England get update on at-risk World Cup game as agreement reached in £5.8m stadium row
Football.content:
England get update on at-risk World Cup game as agreement reached in £5.8m stadium row

A statement has been issued after concerns over a stadium set to host an England World Cup fixture in the United States this summer

View 2 Images The 2026 World Cup has endured several problems in the build-up (Image: PA)

England's 2026 World Cup fixture against Ghana will go ahead in Foxborough, Boston, after initial concerns about $7.8million (£5.8m) security costs. The Gillette Stadium in the Massachusetts town is due to host seven World Cup games this summer, including the Three Lions' Group L clash with Ghana.

‌



There were concerns that the town had demanded an up-front payment of security funds, with the stadium only being granted a license if police and all security measures were paid for. The row saw Foxborough up against both the tournament organisers and a company headed by Robert Krath, owner of the New England Patriots, with the games played at their stadium.

‌



But a joint statement, via the Athletic, has now been distributed by Kraft Sports & Entertainment (KSE), Boston Soccer 2026 and the Town of Foxborough to confirm that an agreement has been reached.

‌



Tottenham star Antonin Kinsky breaks silence after Champions League 'nightmare' READ MORE :

Chelsea capitulate at PSG as blunder triggers collapse and leaves Blues' hopes in tatters READ MORE :

It said the three parties “have reached an understanding collectively that will allow Boston Soccer 2026 and the Town of Foxborough to finalise the details needed to approve an event license at the March 17 public hearing and ensure a safe and successful FIFA World Cup 2026 tournament at Gillette Stadium this summer.”

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

It means that while a license is yet to be issued, the broad terms look to have been agreed.

‌



The statement adds: “As part of this arrangement, the Town of Foxborough will not incur any cost or financial burden related to the FIFA World Cup, with Boston Soccer 2026 providing advance funding for security-related capital expenditures and the full extent of deployment that public safety officials have determined is needed to execute the event with Kraft Sports + Entertainment’s backing.”

View 2 Images The Gillette Stadium will host one England group match and two Scotland games (Image: 2026 Brooke Sutton)

Foxborough town chair Bill Yukna credited a personal intervention by Kraft with helping to resolve the dispute.

‌



He said: “On behalf of the Town of Foxborough, I’m very pleased to say that all of our funding concerns have been addressed.

“I want to personally thank KSE and Robert Kraft for his involvement in bringing the funding concerns to a resolution. We expect that any open issues in the license will be resolved before the 17th public hearing and we look forward to a very successful and safe World Cup event.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

Article continues below

"I also want to thank our Police Chief Michael Grace, our Fire Chief Michael Kelleher and Town Manager Paige Duncan for their many hours and dedication to getting us to the finish line in this issue.”

The tournament in Mexico, Canada and the United States has had a chaotic build-up with just three months to go until the opening game between Mexico and South Africa on June 11. Iran have already threatened to withdraw from the event, while Iraq’s playoff is also in major doubt due to travel issues in the Middle East amid the ongoing conflict in the region.

Football.title: Who can replace Iran at the 2026 World Cup? FIFA rules explained
Football.content:
Who can replace Iran at the 2026 World Cup? FIFA rules explained

FIFA face the prospect of having to find a team for the World Cup with Iran outlining their position to boycott the tournament given their conflict with the USA

View 2 Images Iran have qualified for the World Cup, but have no intention of participating (Image: ATTA KENARE/AFP via Getty Images)

FIFA could be facing a race against time to find a replacement for Iran - after the nation claimed "under no circumstances ⁠can we ⁠participate in ⁠the World Cup" following the US-Israeli airstrikes on their country.

‌



The tournament in North America is predominantly being played in the USA, and their political decisions have caused ripples for the footballing world. The conflict killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and destroyed parts of the Iranian capital, Tehran.

‌



Iran’s sports minister has laid out the nation's plans to bypass the World Cup, meaning FIFA need to find a new country to go into a group with Belgium, Egypt and New Zealand. Iran had qualified top of their section in the Asian qualification system, but world football's bosses have several options available to them.

‌



The FIFA rulebook states a replacement will come from: “A nominated alternate, often the direct runner-up from the relevant qualifying playoff or highest-ranked non-qualified team from that confederation.”

One solution would be to fast-track the UAE into the competition proper, given they finished behind Iran in qualification. However, they lost to Iraq during the Asian confederation’s match that decided the intercontinental play-off, which is set to take place in a few weeks.

World Cup crisis deepens as row over England venue continues READ MORE :

Iran punishments for World Cup withdrawal and can FIFA replace them with another nation? READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



Iraq will play the winner of Bolivia and Suriname for a spot at the World Cup, which has actually complicated the saga. There is talk of FIFA handing Iraq an automatic spot in the tournament as Iran’s replacement - leaving the UAE to instead feature in the intercontinental play-off.

A unlikely workaround for FIFA would be to play a World Cup group with just three teams, leaving Belgium, Egypt and New Zealand to play against each other. The expanded tournament does mean that the best performing third-placed side will advance into the knockouts - but having a group with only three teams complicates that scenario.

View 2 Images FIFA boss Gianni Infantino has received assurances from Donald Trump that Iran are still welcome - but the likelihood is he will need to find another nation (Image: Emilee Chinn - FIFA, FIFA via Getty Images)

‌



FIFA President Gianni Infantino has met with US President Donald Trump and claimed he received assurances that Iran are welcome at the tournament despite the conflict in the Middle East.

He said: "During the discussions, President Trump reiterated that the Iranian team is, of course, welcome to compete in the tournament in the United States."

Iran’s sports minister, Ahmad Donyamali, has made their stance crystal clear as he fired a shot at the current US office. He said during a television interview: "Considering that this corrupt ⁠regime [the US] has ⁠assassinated our leader, under no circumstances ⁠can we ⁠participate in ⁠the World Cup. In view of the malicious measures taken against Iran, two wars were forced upon us within eight or nine months, and several thousand of our people were killed. Our children are not safe and, fundamentally, such conditions for participation do not exist.”

Football.title: Concerning Trent Alexander-Arnold trait picked up on by Spanish media despite Real Madrid win
Football.content:
Concerning Trent Alexander-Arnold trait picked up on by Spanish media despite Real Madrid win

Spanish media have had their say on Trent Alexander-Arnold's performance for Real Madrid against Manchester City

View 2 Images Trent Alexander-Arnold started for Real Madrid against Manchester City in the Champions League (Image: NurPhoto, NurPhoto via Getty Images)

Trent Alexander-Arnold relished Real Madrid's triumph over Manchester City on Wednesday evening. The Spanish team stormed into a 3-0 lead at the Bernabeu, making an impeccable start to their Champions League round of 16 tie.

‌



Manager Alvaro Arbeloa selected Alexander-Arnold in his starting line-up, and the ex-Liverpool full-back would have been thrilled by Fede Valverde's first-half hat-trick. The Uruguayan midfielder garnered all the praise, but Spanish media had a mixed response to Alexander-Arnold's performance.

‌



His one-on-one duel with City winger Jeremy Doku emerged as a concern. Pep Guardiola's squad were unable to better the early scoreline, but the right-back's attempts to stifle Doku attracted criticism.

‌



However, the England international was commended for stepping up his game after half-time. The 3-0 outcome leaves City with a massive deficit to overturn ahead of the second leg at the Etihad Stadium next week.

FOLLOW OUR REAL MADRID FB PAGE! Latest news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Alexander-Arnold will be hoping to keep his place as Los Blancos aim to secure their place in the quarter-finals. Here's what the Spanish press thought about the former Liverpool star's performance.

‌



Real Madrid destroy Man City as Federico Valverde nets stunning Champions League hat-trick READ MORE :

Jude Bellingham's behaviour in Real Madrid stands says everything as Man City implode READ MORE :

AS

Spanish outlet AS dubbed Alexander-Arnold as being "by far the weakest link" in Madrid's defence. The report claimed that the 27-year-old was "unable to contain Doku", forcing Valverde to help out.

He was also described as appearing "somewhat nervous and imprecise in possession". While that would represent a shift from his typical performance, Alexander-Arnold was commended for a clever chip shortly before being substituted in the 83rd minute.

Sport

The Spanish publication awarded him a six out of 10 in their player ratings. Sport characterised the defender as "one of Madrid's key players in building attacks".

‌



View 2 Images Alexander-Arnold was up against Manchester City winger Jeremy Doku (Image: Alberto Gardin/Eurasia Sport Images/Getty Images)

However, the report noted that he "struggled defensively" against Doku. The City forward was praised for his attacking approach against the former Liverpool man.

Sport stated that Alexander-Arnold improved in the second half. Meanwhile, Doku's influence waned after a promising start.

‌



Marca

Marca also scored the full-back a six out of 10 for his performance. The report echoed similar criticism of Alexander-Arnold struggling against Doku in the opening half.

The assessment of his first 45 minutes read: "Too much work on defence to shine on offence." However, that altered after the interval.

The publication suggested he reached "a truce" with Doku and improved defensively. Alexander-Arnold was credited with becoming "more solid" after overcoming the winger's early dominance.

Football.title: Premier League cult hero's stunning daughter follows in his footsteps vs England
Football.content:
Premier League cult hero's stunning daughter follows in his footsteps vs England

He spent years in the Premier League and is now watching his talented daughter forge her own path

View 3 Images Ida Marin Hermannsdottir [L] has followed in her father's footsteps (Image: The FA via Getty Images)

The daughter of former Premier League star Hermann Hreidarsson has earned her first international caps. Hreidarsson was a fan favourite during stints at Portsmouth, Charlton and Ipswich, while earning 89 caps for Iceland.

‌



The apple hasn't fallen far from the tree, as his daughter, Ida Marin Hermannsdottir, has now also pulled on the national team shirt at senior level. Ida Marin, 23, made her Iceland debut against Spain last week before appearing again in her nation's 2-0 loss to England women at Nottingham's City Ground, where her dad turned out numerous times during his playing days.

‌



Ida Marin, a midfielder, completed the full 90 minutes against Spain and the opening 63 minutes against England, with her proud father was watching from the stands.

‌



She began her career at Icelandic club Fylkir before joining Valur and, in 2024, FH (Fimleikafelag Hafnarfjordur), where her impressive performances earned her an international call-up.

Man Utd handed timely injury boost for Aston Villa game after key player's setback READ MORE :

Harry Redknapp explains Tottenham return stance as Igor Tudor faces sack deadline READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Off the pitch, Ida Marin has cultivated a substantial following on Instagram, where she boasts more than 7,000 followers. Alongside her footballing achievements, the blonde enjoys sharing glimpses from her personal life with friends and family.

‌



She is one of Hermann's four children. Ida Marin and her sister, Thelma Loa, are from his first marriage to Ragna Loa Stefansdottir, whilst he has two sons with his fiancée, Alexandra Fanney Johannsdottir.

View 3 Images Ida Marin has a growing social media following (Image: ida_marin/Instagram)

Iceland's women have been drawn in a 'group of death' alongside European champions England and World Cup winners Spain. The Group A victors will secure automatic qualification for the 2027 tournament in Brazil, while the remaining three nations will enter the play-offs alongside teams from Groups B and C.

‌



Hermann, a tough-tackling defender, enjoyed a 15-year career in English football after joining Crystal Palace in 1997/98. He subsequently turned out for Brentford and Wimbledon before racking up over 100 appearances for Ipswich, Charlton and Portsmouth.

View 3 Images Former Portsmouth defender Hermann Hreidarsson was in the stands as his daughter faced England (Image: Getty Images)

His English football journey concluded with a short stint at Coventry City. He headed back to Iceland in 2013, taking on player-manager roles at IBV and Fylkir and having a short period managing Fylkir's women's side.

Article continues below

Hermann made his return to English football as assistant to Sol Campbell at struggling League One outfit Southend United in October 2019. After being unable to prevent the Shrimpers' drop to League Two, both he and Campbell left by mutual agreement.

Football.title: Sean Dyche emerges as shock candidate for next Tottenham boss if Spurs sack Igor Tudor
Football.content:
Sean Dyche emerges as shock candidate for next Tottenham boss if Spurs sack Igor Tudor

Igor Tudor's immediate future at Tottenham looks to have been decided, but Spurs are planning for the worst case scenario and have identified Sean Dyche has a target

View 2 Images Seach Dyche would a leading contender for the Tottenham job (Image: Jon Hobley/MI News/NurPhoto via Getty Images)

Sean Dyche has emerged as a shock candidate to become Tottenham's next manager - but Spurs are sticking with Igor Tudor for now. Croatian boss Tudor has lost all four of his games in charge, leaving Tottenham hovering one point above the relegation zone in the Premier League.

‌



Spurs' Champions League campaign is doing little to remedy their perilous domestic situation, having lost their round-of-16 first leg away to Atletico Madrid 5-2 on Tuesday. They'll face Liverpool at 4:30pm on Sunday and by that time could find themselves in the bottom three.

‌



Tudor is expected be in the dugout at Anfield but a fifth consecutive defeat could spell the end for the ex-Juventus manager after less than a month in North London. The Sun report that should Spurs pull the trigger, Dyche would be a leading contender.

‌



Having been sacked by Nottingham Forest a month ago, the former Everton, Burnley and Watford boss is available. Dyche is said to be aware of Tottenham's interest and would become their second interim appointment this calendar year.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Tottenham star Antonin Kinsky breaks silence after Champions League 'nightmare' READ MORE :

Harry Redknapp explains Tottenham return stance as Igor Tudor faces sack deadline READ MORE :

Speaking last week on The Football Boardroom podcast, Dyche revealed that he's had offers from 'top-level' clubs. "I know what I am. I could branch off this minute now and do what you're doing now and be quite happy," the 54-year-old explained.

‌



"You know, I've done my bit so if I do more, great. If I don't do more, I won't be crying over it. I won't be, you know, but you're left with this moment or that moment.

"Okay, yeah, not bothered about that. I've given my lot, I always do. So I've got no problem with that. So if I choose to step away, it will be my choice. It won't be because no one will employ me.

Should Tottenham sack Igor Tudor? Have your say in the comments section.

‌



View 2 Images Igor Tudor is expected to be in charge against Liverpool on Sunday (Image: Denis Doyle/Getty Images)

"I could certainly, I've had offers already by the way, so that's not a problem. Top level [offers] but the point is, you go, 'Do I need that? Do I want to make a decision? Not at the moment."

Tottenham could force his hand, though, despite their crisis which saw Thomas Frank axed in February. They've now lost six games in a row across all competitions for the first time in their 143-year history.

Article continues below

After facing Liverpool, Spurs will need a miracle when they host Atletico in the second leg on Wednesday. Following that is a relegation six-pointer at home to Forest next Sunday.

Football.title: Wales unchanged for Italy as they look to end losing Six Nations streak
Football.content:
Wales boss Steve Tandy has challenged his players to "go up another level" after naming an unchanged side for their Guinness Six Nations finale against Italy.

Although bottom of the table and still searching for a first win of the tournament, Tandy has given a vote of confidence to the starting XV that pushed Ireland all the way in Dublin last week.

Wales were within one score until the 76th minute before losing 27-17 to Andy Farrell's side.

"Having an unchanged team, I think it reflects the cohesion and the consistency in the group and the consistency of performance out in Ireland," said Tandy.

"This week we need to go up another level. We've had some really good performances. I think there were parts against France, then we built up into the Scotland game where I thought we were very good.

"Against Ireland we stepped up to the plate physically. Now it's putting it all together and creating our best performance this weekend."

Image: Steve Tandy hopes Wales' performance against Ireland will give them a boost

Blair Murray's return is the only change to the matchday squad, with the Scarlets full-back replacing Bath winger Louie Hennessey on the bench.

Wales are seeking a first Six Nations win for 1,099 days, their last victory coming away to Italy in March 2023.

They have not won a home match in the tournament since beating Scotland in February 2022.

"For the team and myself, going back to Principality Stadium is amazing. The boys did really well creating their own energy against Ireland, but there's nothing better (than being at home)," Tandy added.

"The boys reference in meetings what the Scotland game meant to them and the French game, the New Zealand game - the fans have been incredible.

"I think they're really behind the team and the team are really putting in performances to represent our nation."

Football.title: Russell: Ferrari being 'selfish and silly' over F1 start procedure
Football.content:
Mercedes driver George Russell has accused Ferrari of blocking the FIA from making changes to Formula 1's starting procedure for this weekend's Chinese Grand Prix and being "selfish" and "a little bit silly" for doing so.

Russell was victorious at the season-opening Australian Grand Prix on Sunday as he and team-mate Kimi Antonelli converted a front-row lockout to a one-two at the chequered flag, but both had to recover from losing places at the start.

A factor in their slow getaways was that both, along with several other drivers, were left with low levels of battery after the formation lap, having been caught out by a rule that Russell says disadvantages those who have qualified on the front half of the grid.

Russell claims the sport's governing body, the FIA, wants to change the rules to eliminate the issue, but that Ferrari, who made superb starts in Melbourne as Charles Leclerc took the lead and Lewis Hamilton surged forward, are unwilling to provide the 'super majority' required to push through the alteration.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Charles Leclerc and Lewis Hamilton's Ferrari's rocket away at the start of the Australian GP

"Unfortunately, sometimes when you're trying to make changes for the good of the sport, if a team has a competitive edge - like Ferrari at the moment with the race starts - they wouldn't wish to see anything changing," Russell told Sky Sports F1.

"I think half the grid got caught out by a quirk in the rules for the race start in Melbourne. We now know that, but because there was some resistance from some teams to change, we're just going to have to work around it."

Speaking to the written media earlier on Thursday in Shanghai, Russell accused Ferrari of being "selfish" and "a bit silly".

He said: "The FIA was looking to potentially adjust that (the start procedure), but as you can imagine some teams who were making good starts didn't want it, which I think is just a little bit silly. I'm not overly concerned, but it's definitely a challenge."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player George Russell believes Mercedes has the quickest car but admits he's cautious of other teams' upgrades this season

Asked to clarify whether the FIA wants to change the rule, Russell added: "They could do. I think they want to, but they need a super majority from the teams, which they don't have. So, you can probably guess which team is against that.

"I don't think their gain is coming from this issue. Now all the teams know the problem, we'll just drive around it. But it's just creating a bit of unnecessary complications to something that doesn't really need to be there. Half the grid messed up in Melbourne. We'll adjust. We know what we need to be wary of now.

"The FIA did just want to make our life easier and just remove this harvest limit, but, as often [happens], people have selfish views and they want to do what's best for themselves. And that's part of Formula 1 and part of the challenge of Formula 1. We'll deal with it and I think the starts here will be much better."

What is the controversial rule?

The dispute has arisen amid the sport's adjustment to both new power units and chassis for the 2026 season, following the biggest regulation change in F1 history.

The new power units, which rely upon more electrical energy than their predecessors, have made energy management the most crucial element in extracting performance at this early stage of the regulations.

Russell explained: "I think there was an error that caught a lot of teams out, which was the harvest limit on the formation lap.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Ride on board with Lewis Hamilton and Charles Leclerc as they both get a rocketing start off the line in Australia

"So, a very quirky rule… every lap there's a harvest limit. The drivers who started in the first half of the grid, who were beyond the timing line, they were already within that lap. So when you did your formation-lap start, you're spending your battery and you're charging your battery, which goes towards your harvest limit.

"The drivers at the back, when they did their formation lap start, they then launch away, they cross the start-finish line and then it resets because they're effectively on the next lap.

"So, from what we did in the practice starts, we did the launch before this line, and it reset. And on the race start, starting from pole, I went on the throttle, I charged the battery, but it took like 50 per cent of my harvest limit of that lap, so when I got halfway around the track, I could no longer charge the battery, I had no power to do proper burnouts."

Hamilton: I haven't caught up with the group chat

Before Russell's accusation, Hamilton said he thought the start procedure was "exciting to watch" and that it would get better with time.

"There's obviously the group chat, but I haven't caught up with the group chat," said Hamilton when asked if there had been discussion among the drivers. "So I'm not really sure what they're all saying on it."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Lewis Hamilton believes the Mercedes pace will be tough to beat this weekend at the Chinese GP

"I'm not really into too many group chats because you're always playing catch up.

"I mean it's exciting to watch but I think it's just the first race, so I think it's just inevitable that there are issues along the way. But I think it will just get better and better."

Football.title: Hamilton hails Ferrari as radical 'macarena' wing set to debut
Football.content:
Ferrari have brought their innovative rotating rear wing - being dubbed the 'macarena' - to the Chinese Grand Prix with Lewis Hamilton proud the team are "pushing and chasing" for upgrades so early in the season.

The Scuderia caused a stir on the penultimate day of testing in Bahrain last month when they debuted the unique rear-wing design on their SF-26 car.

When the car is in straight-line mode, instead of the usual method of the top element of the rear wing flattening to reduce drag, Ferrari's design rotates into an upside-down position.

The design was not brought to last week's season-opener in Australia but was spotted in the pit lane on the car ahead of this weekend's Sprint weekend at the Shanghai International Circuit, which features one of the longest straights on the calendar at 1.1km where straight-line speed is key.

"We did a full day or so on the wing, so I think we got all the running needed for it," said Hamilton of its initial running in testing.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Lewis Hamilton believes the Mercedes pace will be tough to beat this weekend at the Chinese GP

"And I'm so grateful for the team too, because it was actually supposed to be later down the line and they worked really hard to develop it and get it brought here.

"So it's great to see that the team are fighting, the team are pushing and chasing and really working overtime back at the factory to be able to bring upgrades, because that's the name of the game.

"I think last year I didn't get to see the team's full potential in that mode, because we were focused on this year's cars."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Check out Ferrari's innovative upside-down rear wing during the second day of the final test in Bahrain

On whether he had felt any difference when running the wing - which is now being called the 'macarena' after the 1990s dance sensation - in Bahrain, Hamilton said: "Unfortunately, it did the same, we just see it in the mirror.

"So I'm looking forward to seeing what it will do here."

'Not an impossible gap to close' - will Ferrari be closer to Mercedes in China?

Ferrari arrive in Shanghai aiming to build on a competitive start to F1's new era last week in Melbourne, where they finished third and fourth behind the Mercedes drivers.

While the Scuderia's drivers push eventual race winner George Russell hard in the opening stages of the Melbourne race before strategies diverged after a Virtual Safety Car, Hamilton says his old team's qualifying advantage of 0.8s showed the outright gap Ferrari have to close.

"We knew that Mercedes were looking really strong but little did we know it was as big a gap that we saw," said Hamilton.

"It's not an impossible gap to close but just it's going to take all hands on deck and I'm confident in my team doing everything back at the factory to try and close that gap."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Listen in as Charles Leclerc compares his epic duel with George Russell to the mushroom from Mario Kart.

Hamilton - who won last year's Sprint race in China in what remains the best result of his Ferrari career so far - played down their chances of beating Mercedes this weekend but was hopeful of another step forward.

"It's far to early to say and, secondly, we were eight tenths behind in qualifying in the last race," he added.

"We haven't added eight tenths of performance to the car in four days. I think it will still be very tough to beat Mercedes this weekend. You also have to assume the others are going to be picking up pace - like McLaren and the second Red Bull being in the fight as well.

"We'll just focus on doing the best job we can and extracting the most from the car."

He added: "It's not eight tenths but we could hopefully extract a bit more from the car this weekend. So I'm really excited for the weekend."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Ride on board with Lewis Hamilton and Charles Leclerc as they both get a rocketing start off the line in Australia

Team-mate Charles Leclerc, who engaged in a thrilling early ding-dong duel for the lead with Russell last Sunday, believes the longer race distance does, however, again offer Ferrari their best chance to challenge the Silver Arrows at the moment.

"In qualifying it's going to take a lot of work in order to change the advantage that they have," said Leclerc.

"Eight tenths in Melbourne was absolutely huge, I don't expect it to be much closer here.

"Closer for sure, there are things we are optimising that we haven't optimise in Melbourne, so it will get better. But they still have a significant advantage.

"In the race we are closer so I hope starting from this weekend we can put them under a bit more pressure."

Football.title: Verstappen positive about talks with F1 bosses over rules changes
Football.content:
Max Verstappen says he is engaged in talks with Formula 1 and the FIA over how to make improvements to the sport's new rules.

Verstappen has been a vocal critic of the 2026 regulations, which feature a 50-50 split between internal combustion and electrical power and require more energy deployment strategies, and stated that he is not enjoying driving the latest cars as much as the previous generation.

That has cast doubt about the four-time world champion's long-term future in the sport.

But speaking in the Drivers' Press Conference ahead of this weekend's second round in China, Verstappen - who this week confirmed his participation in the Nurburgring 24 Hours between grands prix dates in May - said he was nonetheless "conflicted" about the situation and was engaged in helping the sport's bosses make refinements for the future.

"I don't want to leave, really," said Verstappen, whose Red Bull contract runs to 2028 but is thought to contain break clauses.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights from the 2026 Australian Grand Prix

"I wish I had a bit more fun, for sure, but I'm also doing other stuff that is a lot of fun. I get to race the Nordschleife. I hope in the coming years I get to do Spa [24 Hours], hopefully Le Mans.

"So I'm combining stuff to find other stuff I find really fun as well. I have my [Verstappen Racing] team going on, so I have a lot of distractions at the same time - positive distractions, I would call it.

"But at the same time it's a bit conflicting because I don't really enjoy driving the car, but I do enjoy working with all the people in the team and from the engine department as well. It's almost like a bit of a mind… I can't swear, it's 5K [fine] now!

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Speaking on The F1 Show Podcast, Bernie Collins previews the Chinese Grand Prix and why this Sprint weekend will bring 'all sorts of chaos'

"I don't want to leave so I also hope of course it gets better. I've had discussions with F1 and the FIA and we are working towards something and hopefully that will improve everything."

On the nature of those discussions with the sport's bosses, Verstappen said: "I definitely hope not [that it's the same] for the next few years. I hope already for next year we can make a decent improvement.

"There are a few options that we're discussing."

Verstappen: Red Bull 'cannot fight' with Mercedes, Ferrari yet

Verstappen finished sixth at last weekend's season-opening Australian Grand Prix in a strong recovery drive after a crash in qualifying had left him 20th on the grid.

He believes Red Bull's car and engine has "a lot of potential" to exploit yet but admits the presence of a gap to the Mercedes and Ferrari cars, which finished in the top-four positions in Melbourne, is clear.

"I think if I would have started, let's say, a little bit up front, I think the best that I could have done was one spot higher because we didn't have the pace of the top four cars," he said.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Red Bull's Max Verstappen crashed into the barriers during Q1 at the Australian Grand Prix

"We also had a lot of degradation and graining. We'll see what happens in the coming races, if we can close that gap a little bit."

Asked if this weekend's race in China would prove a better circuit for Red Bull, Verstappen replied: "Honestly, it's such a jungle out there at the moment. I think that it's very hard to really know.

"I mean, I would hope that it gets a bit closer, not even bigger than the gap that it was in Melbourne, but it's clear that at the moment we cannot fight with those cars."

Football.title: Draper stuns Djokovic to reach Indian Wells quarter-finals
Football.content:
Britain's Jack Draper fought back to stun Novak Djokovic to keep his Indian Wells title defence alive after a thrilling victory.

Draper came through a gripping match lasting more than two-and-a-half hours in a deciding tiebreak to win 4-6 6-4 7-6 7-5) as he beat Djokovic for the first time in his career.

The 24-year-old will face Daniil Medvedev in the last eight on Thursday night, live on Sky Sports Tennis, at midnight before compatriot Cameron Norrie faces Carlos Alcaraz.

"Just an incredible feeling," said Draper, who only returned to the ATP Tour in late February following eight months out with injury.

"I'm out here against Novak, to me the greatest tennis player there is, and someone I've been admiring and watching since I was a little kid.

"So to do that, I was just incredibly proud of myself. It gives me so much confidence. I still don't feel like I'm playing anywhere near the way I want to play.

"I came out here tonight and I won that match through determination and trying to problem solve and do my best and have a great attitude."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Jack Draper reacts to his win over Novak Djokovic, and his confidence as his recovery from injury

How Draper came back to beat Djokovic

Djokovic took a hard-fought opening set 6-4 but Draper responded by taking a second break point in the opening game of the second.

The Serbian broke back and Draper appeared in trouble in the eighth game when he was pegged back from 40-0 to deuce before eventually holding.

A powerful forehand winner down the line in the next earned Draper three break points at 0-40 and he broke with the first when Djokovic netted. Djokovic refused to roll over and saved two set points before an ace finally drew the left-hander level.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Novak Djokovic was left lying on the floor after an incredible 26-shot rally with Jack Draper at Indian Wells

Djokovic began to look exhausted in the decider as a number of gruelling points took their toll, and having held serve in a riveting opening game that featured a 26-shot rally, he found himself 0-30 down in his next service game before Draper took another lengthy exchange to earn two break points.

The 24-time Grand Slam champion regularly leaned on his racquet between points as Draper broke but the Briton was unable to see the contest out as, serving for match, he hit a wild backhand wide, double-faulted and prodded a tame drop shot into net, with a magnificent Djokovic drop shot seeing him take the second break point.

Djokovic sent a backhand long to hand Draper a first match point in the tie-break and netted a backhand to hand the Briton one of the most significant wins of his career.

"I'm a bit overwhelmed. Just to be here at Indian Wells is a blessing after the hell I went through with the arm injury I had," Draper told Sky Sports Tennis.

"I still feel like I'm struggling with the way I want to play. I'm struggling with my confidence in my game. I'm trying to problem-solve and be more aggressive. I was waiting for him to miss a lot of the time.

"I picked it up a bit in the second and third [sets]. I tried to put more pace on the ball and Novak is someone who won't just give it you. I had a lot of will power and determination, so hopefully I can get better from here."

Henman on Draper's 'unbelievable' win Sky Sports Tennis' Tim Henman



“Unbelievable performance - a real rollercoaster. The first two sets, they each had their moments and the early part of the third was absolutely vital. Draper, took the match by the scruff of the neck. He got the break. Djokovic looked like he was cooked. He was absolutely exhausted out there.



“Jack had the opportunity at 5-4, to serve the match, and by his own admission, said he completely choked, and we've all been there, but the way he was able to regroup and get that tiebreak was a phenomenal effort.”

Kartal retires due to back problem against Rybakina

Earlier, Britain's Sonay Kartal finally succumbed to her ongoing back problems as her run at Indian Wells ended against Elena Rybakina in the last 16.

The British No 2 had defied pain in her back to record impressive wins over Emma Navarro and Madison Keys but, despite competing gamely against the world number three, retired while trailing 6-4 4-3.

Kartal was broken in the third game of the match and went two breaks down after a video replay showed her top had made contact with the net at break point in the fifth.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights of the Indian Wells match between Sonay Kartal and Elena Rybakina

However, Kartal rallied to get a break back despite needing treatment from the physio between games and put the Rybakina serve under pressure before the Kazakhstani held to take the first set.

The second set was going on serve until Kartal stretched for a return while break point down at 3-3 and, in clear discomfort, made her way to the net to shake hands.

"Definitely not the way I wanted to finish the match, of course (I wish her a) speedy recovery," said reigning Australian Open champion Rybakina.

"It's not easy, these kind of injuries. It was a tough match anyway. I'm glad I'm through to the next one."

Football.title: Valverde's first-half hat-trick leaves Man City on brink of CL exit
Football.content:
Federico Valverde's brilliant first-half hat-trick secured Real Madrid a stunning 3-0 victory over Manchester City, who have a mountain to climb to reach the Champions League quarter-finals.

Real Madrid, who were missing the likes of Kylian Mbappe, Jude Bellingham and Rodrygo, were distinct second-favourites heading into the game but they produced a clinical opening 45 minutes as Valverde stole the show at the Bernabeu.

Thibaut Courtois's long pass over the head of Nico O'Reilly was exquisitely controlled by Valverde as his first touch took him in on goal. He rounded Gianluigi Donnarumma, who had charged out to the edge of his penalty area, before sliding the ball it home from an extremely tight angle to give the hosts the lead (20).

The goal had come somewhat against the run of play after what was a fast start from City but Real Madrid took full control of the game when Valverde popped up behind the City defence to latch onto Vinicius Jnr's flicked pass and finish beyond Donnarumma again (27).

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Sky Sports reporter Vinny O'Connor rounds up a disappointing night for Pep Guardiola after Manchester City are beaten 3-0 by Real Madrid in the Champions League.

The first half was the Valverde show and he capped a phenomenal performance with arguably his best goal of the night. He showed an outstanding touch to bring the ball down inside the penalty area and flick it past Marc Geuhi. Donnarumma rushed out to make a block but Valverde flashed a shot past him (42) for a first career hat-trick and to put the hosts in dreamland.

Image: Valverde scored a first career hat-trick

After Real Madrid's devastating first-half performance, Manchester City's odds to win the Champions League had gone from 9/1 to 33/1 but they could have lengthened further had it not been for Donnarumma in the second half.

The City goalkeeper had to be alert to keep out Brahim Diaz's low drive minutes after the restart as City threatened to crumble.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Michael Brown reacts to Federico Valverde's hat-trick in Real Madrid's Champions League clash with Manchester City

Donnarumma then atoned for his initial error after he brought down Vinicius Jnr, who was through on goal. The Italian recovered by keeping out the Brazilian's lacklustre penalty in a moment City hoped might swing some momentum back in their favour.

However, Pep Guardiola's side found Courtois in inspired form, the former Chelsea goalkeeper showing fine reactions to divert O'Reilly's tackle-shot over the crossbar with his leg to leave City's Champions League hopes hanging by a thread ahead of next week's second leg at the Etihad.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player The Soccer Special pundit, Michael Brown, says Real Madrid punished Pep Guardiola’s adventurous set-up in a disappointing Champions League night.

Pep: Man City have 'not much' chance against Real but will always try

Guardiola conceded Man City had "not much" chance but refused to rule out a dramatic fightback in the second leg next week.

"It is a bad result, we cannot deny it," Guardiola said.

"The first goal was not well defended and after that the quality they had with Valverde and the other players, it was a difficult result.

"But obviously 3-0 is better than 4-0. We have six days. We will recover, go to West Ham, then with our people we will try."

Asked what chance City had, Guardiola added: "Now, not much but I'm not a guy to say we're not going to try.

"Now it is most difficult (moment) to live but our mindset is we will look (at) what to do better, try to be more active in the final third and we will try."

Pep: We were better than the result Man City manager Pep Guardiola to TNT Sports:



"I had a feeling that we were better than the result said...but the result is here. We have one week and then we will see them at the Etihad."



On his side's chances of mounting a comeback, he added: "Now we recover mentally and physically and go to West Ham. We will see. We will be with our people - I'm pretty sure they will come - and in football you never know. We will try."

'Defeat feels really dark'

Manchester City midfielder Bernardo Silva, speaking to TNT Sports:

"It is difficult to speak about it because I would need to watch it again. My feeling on the pitch was that we started the game well.

"The environment we could not control and I think my team let the emotions change the game. We felt comfortable and were finding the right spaces but after conceding the first one we lost completely the control, we stopped controlling transitions and second balls. When you play against Real Madrid with the quality they have you pay the price.

"It is a tough stadium to play at. We are used to playing at these stadiums because playing at Anfield and St James' Park is very tough. You need to go through these moments to become better and today was just a learning game for the whole team.

"It is quite frustrating [we didn't score], even though we believe it is still possible because in football a lot of things can happen, at half-time we were saying, 'the situation is bad' there were a lot of things that shouldn't have happened but with one goal you are in the tie.

At 3-0 it makes it a bit more difficult. Now it feels really bad, now it feels really dark. Tomorrow is another day and for sure next week we will go to the game thinking we have a chance.

"Right now is the moment to reflect and see what we didn't do right. Then next week when we are preparing we will be hopeful we can do something."
